title: super-worktree Workspace Configuration
description: Workspace-level config that declares a parent folder containing multiple sibling git repos as a coordinated unit. Allows a single feature to span multiple repos with one branch name.

version.description: Workspace schema version. Current build expects 1.

name.description: Display name for the workspace (defaults to parent dir basename).

projects.description: Each child project (sibling git repo) participating in the workspace.

alias.description: Short identifier; used in --projects flag and symlink hub.

path.description: Path to the project's git repo, relative to workspace root or absolute.

defaultBase.description: Per-project default base ref (overrides workspace --base when set).

defaultProjects.description: Aliases used when --projects is omitted. If empty, all projects are selected.

branchTemplate.description: Template for branch name. Supports {ticket}, {slug}, {feature} placeholders.

symlinkLayer.description: Create symlink hub at <workspace>/.worktrees/<feature>/<alias>. Disable for Windows without dev-mode.

rollback.description: On partial-create failure: strict = remove already-created siblings; leave = keep them.

hooks.description: Workspace-scoped hooks. Run with cwd=WORKSPACE_ROOT and env: FEATURE, PROJECTS, WORKSPACE_ROOT, WORKTREE_ROOT_DIR.

sync.description: Workspace-level default sync settings; per-project overrides take precedence.
